agent.name: ux-flow-auditor
agent.role: Find broken user flows, inconsistent copy, missing UI states, accessibility gaps, navigation dead ends, and missing error boundaries.
stop_conditions_hit: Sampled top copy, state, and a11y issues; more than 20 copy variants exist across the codebase so coverage_complete is false.

coverage.incomplete_reason: More than 20 copy inconsistencies exist across the codebase; reporting top 10 per Must Do rule #8.

findings.title: Inconsistent Login Terminology
findings.description: The terms 'Sign in', 'Log in', and 'Open login' are used interchangeably across authentication components. Inconsistent CTAs erode trust and confuse users about whether they have an account.

proof_hooks.summary: LoginForm uses 'Log in' while SignupForm uses 'Sign in'
SignupForm uses a different auth term than LoginForm

findings.impact: Users may be confused about authentication state and whether multiple distinct actions exist. Minor erosion of trust and product polish.

suggested_fix.approach: Standardize on 'Log In' (verb) for the action and 'Login' (noun) for the route/page. Apply globally via a shared copy constants file.

history.notes: Discovered inconsistent auth terminology during copy audit of authentication components.

findings.title: Inconsistent Save Terminology
findings.description: 'Save Changes', 'Save', and 'Automatically save changes' are used with varying casing and patterns. While individually acceptable, the inconsistency across surfaces reduces perceived polish.

proof_hooks.summary: Multiple save action variants found in smart-defaults-types.ts

findings.impact: Minor polish issue; users may question whether actions differ semantically. Low user-facing risk.

suggested_fix.approach: Standardize save CTA casing and phrasing in a shared copy constants or i18n file. Use 'Save' for explicit saves and 'Saving…' / 'Saved' for auto-save indicators.

history.notes: Discovered during copy audit of domain type definitions.

findings.title: Placeholder Text in Production
findings.description: Lorem ipsum strings are present in UI weight definitions exposed in production. Placeholder copy that ships to users signals an unfinished product and breaks trust.

proof_hooks.summary: Lorem ipsum placeholder strings found in signatures.ts

findings.impact: Users who encounter lorem ipsum will lose confidence in product quality and completeness. Placeholder text is visually apparent in any rendered UI using these definitions.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace all lorem ipsum strings in src/domain/signatures.ts with representative real copy or clearly named placeholder tokens (e.g. '[Signature preview text]'). Add a lint rule or CI check for 'lorem ipsum' in src/.

history.notes: Discovered lorem ipsum strings in production domain type definitions during copy audit.

findings.title: Missing Error Boundaries on Major Route Components
findings.description: Major sections including ProjectsPage, SettingsPage, and Admin Metrics lack ErrorBoundary wrappers. Only WorkspacePage implements one. An unhandled render error in any unprotected page crashes the entire app for the user.

proof_hooks.summary: App.tsx route setup shows ProjectsPage, SettingsPage, AdminMetrics without ErrorBoundary wrappers
Any unhandled JS error in ProjectsPage propagates to root and shows a blank screen

findings.impact: Any runtime error in an unprotected page causes a full app crash with a blank white screen. Users cannot recover without a hard refresh. Data may appear lost.

suggested_fix.approach: Wrap each top-level route component in App.tsx with an ErrorBoundary. Reuse the pattern from WorkspacePage. Ensure the fallback UI shows a clear error message and a 'Go home' recovery link.
suggested_fix.risk_notes: Low risk. Additive change only. Ensure error boundary fallback does not swallow errors silently — log to monitoring.

history.notes: Confirmed via code inspection of App.tsx route definitions. WorkspacePage has an ErrorBoundary; other major routes do not.

findings.title: Unhelpful Empty States in Workspace
findings.description: The workspace empty state is noted as 'intentional, not instructional' — it provides no guidance to the user on what to do next. First-time users facing an empty canvas have no clear call to action.

proof_hooks.summary: WorkspaceSurface empty state lacks actionable CTA or onboarding copy

findings.impact: New users who land on an empty workspace may feel lost and churn before completing their first flow. Low immediate risk but impacts activation rate.

suggested_fix.approach: Add a simple empty state with a headline, one-sentence description, and a primary CTA button (e.g. 'Start with a template' or 'Add your first step'). Reference the design system empty state pattern if one exists.

history.notes: Discovered during empty-state audit of workspace surface component.

findings.title: Unhelpful Image Alt Text in Design Canvas
findings.description: Images in DesignCanvas use unhelpful alt text such as 'Generated' or bare template literals like '{asset.name}' without providing structural context for screen readers. This fails WCAG 2.1 criterion 1.1.1.

proof_hooks.summary: img elements in DesignCanvas use generic or template-literal alt text

findings.impact: Screen reader users cannot understand image content or purpose. Violates WCAG 2.1 criterion 1.1.1 (Non-text Content). Exposes product to accessibility compliance risk.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace generic alt text with descriptive strings. For AI-generated images, use a structured description (e.g. 'AI-generated image: {asset.description || asset.name}'). For decorative images, use alt='' explicitly.

history.notes: Discovered during accessibility audit of design canvas component images.

findings.title: Undefined Product Voice and Tone
findings.description: The intended product voice and tone are not explicitly defined in the observed design tokens, documentation, or codebase. Without a documented voice guide, copy inconsistencies will continue to accumulate across contributors and AI-generated content.

proof_hooks.summary: No voice/tone documentation found in design tokens, README, or audits/expectations.md

findings.impact: Copy will remain inconsistent over time as the codebase grows and more contributors (human or AI) add surface text. Without a documented standard, fixing copy-mismatch findings will be ad hoc.

suggested_fix.approach: Propose and document a default product voice guide (e.g. clear, concise, professional) in a VOICE.md or audits/expectations.md appendix. Define: tone (formal vs casual), capitalization rules, preferred CTA patterns, and anti-patterns.

history.notes: Raised as a question finding after observing no voice/tone documentation in the codebase or audits configuration.

next_actions.action: Wrap all top-level route components in App.tsx with ErrorBoundary
next_actions.rationale: Prevents full app crashes from unhandled render errors; highest severity finding in this run.

next_actions.action: Remove lorem ipsum placeholder strings from src/domain/signatures.ts
next_actions.rationale: Placeholder copy in production undermines product credibility and is trivial to fix.

next_actions.action: Fix image alt text in DesignCanvas to use descriptive context-aware strings
next_actions.rationale: Accessibility compliance risk; WCAG 2.1 violation with straightforward fix.

next_actions.action: Standardize authentication terminology to a single term ('Log In') across LoginForm and SignupForm
next_actions.rationale: Consistent auth CTAs improve trust; affects the critical first-impression flow.

next_actions.action: Document product voice and tone guide to prevent ongoing copy-mismatch accumulation
next_actions.rationale: Root cause of most copy-mismatch findings; investing in a voice guide prevents recurrence.
